text: The ArtistId in the Artist table is a primary key of type INTEGER.
The Name column in the Artist table allows up to 120 characters and is stored as NVARCHAR.
The AlbumId in the Album table is an auto-incrementing INTEGER identifying each record.
The Title of an album is stored as an NVARCHAR(160) and is a mandatory field.
In the Track table, Milliseconds is an INTEGER representing the song duration.
In the Track table, Bytes is an INTEGER representing the file size on disk.
The UnitPrice in both Track and InvoiceLine is stored as a NUMERIC(10,2) decimal.
The InvoiceDate in the Invoice table is a DATETIME type recording the sale time.
BillingAddress in the Invoice table is an NVARCHAR(70) field.
The Total column in the Invoice table is a NUMERIC(10,2) sum of all linked invoice lines.
FirstName and LastName in the Customer table are NVARCHAR(40) and NVARCHAR(20) respectively.
The Email column in the Customer table is an NVARCHAR(60) and is required for all records.
The ReportsTo column in the Employee table is a nullable INTEGER for management hierarchy.
The BirthDate and HireDate in the Employee table are stored as DATETIME objects.
PostalCode columns across all tables are NVARCHAR(10) to support international formats.
The MediaTypeId in the MediaType table is the unique Primary Key for formats.
The GenreId in the Genre table is the unique Primary Key for music styles.
All Primary Keys in the Chinook schema are defined as NOT NULL by default.
Foreign keys like AlbumId in the Track table are indexed to improve join performance.
The TrackId in the PlaylistTrack table is part of a Composite Primary Key.
The Composer column in the Track table is nullable, as some tracks lack known authors.
The State column in the Customer table is nullable to accommodate countries without states.
The Company column in the Customer table is nullable for individual retail buyers.
The Fax column in both Customer and Employee tables is an optional NVARCHAR(24) field.
The Phone column is an NVARCHAR(24) field available for both Customers and Employees.
The Address column in the Employee table is a required NVARCHAR(70) field.
The Title column in the Employee table describes the job role, such as 'Sales Manager'.
The BillingState in the Invoice table is optional for international shipments.
The Track table has a foreign key GenreId which can be null in some SQL dialects, but is usually populated.
The PlaylistId in the Playlist table is a unique INTEGER primary key.
To find the name of an Artist for a specific Track, you must join Track to Album, then Album to Artist.
A self-join on the Employee table using ReportsTo identifies an employee's manager.
The InvoiceLine table acts as a bridge between the Invoice and the Track tables.
To calculate the total duration of an Album, you sum the Milliseconds of all associated Tracks.
The PlaylistTrack table contains no non-key attributes, only PlaylistId and TrackId.
Joining Customer to Invoice is done via the CustomerId field to track purchasing history.
The MediaType and Genre tables are 'lookup tables' that provide descriptive names for IDs.
To find all tracks in a Playlist by name, you must join Playlist to PlaylistTrack to Track.
An Artist with no entries in the Album table is possible in a relational sense but rare in Chinook data.
The Invoice table's BillingCountry can be used to filter sales by geographic region.
The largest table by record count in the Chinook database is typically the InvoiceLine table.
The smallest tables in Chinook are usually the MediaType and Genre tables.
The Employee table contains exactly one record where ReportsTo is NULL, representing the General Manager.
The Chinook schema does not use triggers for calculating the Invoice Total; it is updated by applications.
Every Track must be assigned a MediaTypeId to define its file format.
The database uses the 'N' prefix in types like NVARCHAR to indicate Unicode support.
The total number of tables in the standard Chinook schema is eleven.
The PlaylistId '1' usually refers to the 'All Music' playlist in the default data.
The InvoiceLineId is a unique surrogate key for every row in the InvoiceLine table.
The UnitPrice in the InvoiceLine table allows for historical price tracking regardless of current Track price.
The Chinook database is often used as a cross-platform alternative to the Northwind database.
The schema supports a one-to-many relationship between Genre and Track.
The schema supports a one-to-many relationship between MediaType and Track.
The schema supports a one-to-many relationship between Album and Track.
The schema supports a one-to-many relationship between Artist and Album.
The schema supports a one-to-many relationship between Customer and Invoice.
The schema supports a one-to-many relationship between Invoice and InvoiceLine.
The schema supports a many-to-many relationship between Playlist and Track via PlaylistTrack.
The Employee hierarchy is a recursive relationship within the same table.
The SupportRepId in the Customer table ensures every customer has a point of contact.
City and Country data is duplicated across Employee, Customer, and Invoice tables for billing accuracy.
The database is designed to handle multiple languages through its NVARCHAR columns.
The Milliseconds to minutes conversion is calculated as Milliseconds divided by 60,000.
The Bytes to Megabytes conversion is calculated as Bytes divided by 1,048,576.
The Genre name 'Bossa Nova' is one of the distinct entries in the Genre table.
The MediaType name 'Purchased AAC audio file' is a standard entry in the default data.
The Employee table's HireDate is usually after the BirthDate for data integrity.
The Invoice table does not contain a 'Tax' column; taxes are assumed to be included in the Total.
The CustomerId is the only link between the retail user and their financial transactions.
Primary keys are usually clustered indexes in the Chinook SQL Server implementation.
The Album title 'For Those About To Rock We Salute You' is associated with ArtistId 1.
The Artist name 'AC/DC' is the first entry in many versions of the Chinook Artist table.
The Track 'For Those About To Rock (We Salute You)' has a length of 343719 milliseconds.
The GenreId for 'Rock' is typically 1 in the Chinook dataset.
The MediaTypeId for 'MPEG audio file' is typically 1 in the Chinook dataset.
The Employee 'Andrew Adams' is often the General Manager in the sample data.
The Employee 'Nancy Edwards' often reports to Andrew Adams as a Sales Manager.
The Customer 'Luís Gonçalves' is a common first record in the Customer table.
The total sales for an individual customer is the sum of 'Total' in the Invoice table for their ID.
The most expensive tracks are those with the highest UnitPrice, usually 1.99 for video content.
The default UnitPrice for a standard song track is 0.99.
The Invoice table's BillingPostalCode may be null for countries that do not use them.
The TrackId is required in the PlaylistTrack table to associate a song with a list.
The PlaylistId is required in the PlaylistTrack table to associate a list with a song.
The Composer field often contains multiple names separated by slashes or commas.
There are no tables for 'Suppliers' or 'Inventory' in the Chinook schema.
The schema is strictly for a digital storefront, not a physical inventory system.
To find the total number of tracks in a genre, you count TrackId grouped by GenreId.
To find the average track length per album, you average Milliseconds grouped by AlbumId.
The InvoiceId is the primary identifier for a single customer purchase event.
The Track table's Name column is not unique; different albums can have tracks with the same name.
The Album table's Title column is not unique, though it is usually unique per Artist.
The Employee's City is where the office or the employee is located, usually Edmonton or Calgary.
The Customer's Country is used for global sales distribution analysis.
The Genre 'Science Fiction' often exists for video content media types.
The MediaType 'Protected MPEG-4 video file' is used for television show tracks.
The Invoice table's Total uses a high-precision decimal to avoid rounding errors.
The ArtistId 22 refers to the artist 'Led Zeppelin' in standard Chinook data.
The AlbumId 122 refers to the album 'Jorge Ben Jor' in standard Chinook data.
The Chinook database provides a clean example of Third Normal Form (3NF) relational design.
